claim: Subagents do not share conversation history with the primary Claude Code agent, so you must output all context they produce as markdown files for other agents to consume.
actionable: When designing subagent workflows, define explicit markdown file outputs that capture all work each subagent does so other agents can read and use them.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: A subagent AI agent factory workflow has these phases: (0) requirements gathering, (1) planning with architecture, (2) parallel agents for prompts/tools/dependencies, (3) implementation, (4) validation with tests.
actionable: Structure your agentic coding projects into these five phases with specialized subagents for each to enable repeatable agent creation.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: The subagent description field is crucial; it tells the primary Claude Code agent when to invoke that subagent, so include clear examples and use cases.
actionable: Write detailed descriptions with examples for each subagent markdown file so Claude Code understands exactly when to invoke them.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: You can select different models (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) for different subagents to optimize for token cost and capability; use Haiku for simpler tasks.
actionable: Assign Haiku models to simple subagent tasks and Opus to complex reasoning tasks to balance cost and capability across your workflow.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: Using slash commands or global rules in claude.md to orchestrate subagents keeps your orchestration logic separate from individual subagent system prompts.
actionable: Define your subagent orchestration workflow in claude.md or as slash commands rather than embedding orchestration logic in individual subagent prompts.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: You can use Archon (knowledge management tool) to give subagents access to external documentation and manage task assignments between agents.
actionable: Connect Archon to Claude Code to provide subagents with curated knowledge from documentation and to manage task distribution across your agent team.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: Context preservation via separated markdown files prevents pollution of the main conversation; web research and knowledge lookups done by subagents don't bloat the primary agent's context.
actionable: Use subagents to isolate expensive operations (web search, knowledge lookup) so the primary agent maintains a clean, focused conversation.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: You can have agents output to an Archon project to store all planning/work artifacts in a database, enabling other people to reference and build on them.
actionable: Configure your agent workflow to output markdown artifacts to an Archon project so the work is centralized and shareable.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent

claim: Always implement a validator subagent that creates unit tests and validates the primary agent's work before delivery; this prevents shipping broken code.
actionable: Include a dedicated validation phase in your subagent workflow where a validator agent writes tests and checks for correctness.
source_title: I Built My Claude Code Subagents DREAM TEAM to Create Any AI Agent
